status_detail: Warning text NOT displayed. Avatar only, no text. Same issue as #476. Root cause: wrtn uses Socket.IO WebSocket for chat, not standard HTTP POST/SSE.

details.behavior: 서포터 fox avatar appears with NO text. Input field not cleared. WebSocket error in console.
details.devtools_network: WebSocket wrtn.socket.io shows status 101. No SSE/POST request for chat visible. WebSocket connection error after keyword message.
details.console_errors: WebSocket connection to wss://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn.socket.io/ failed: invalid. Total 4 errors after test.

details.reason: Test case 1 already confirmed FAIL. Same root cause would apply.

root_cause_analysis.chat_mechanism: Socket.IO WebSocket (NOT HTTP POST/SSE as expected)
root_cause_analysis.problem: Etap APF envelope id=34 (openai_compat_sse) expects HTTP POST with SSE response. wrtn actually uses Socket.IO over WebSocket for chat. The warning injection mechanism designed for SSE streams cannot inject into WebSocket frames.
evidence: Zero HTTP POST requests to api.wrtn.ai during chat
fetch/XHR/EventSource JS interceptors caught no chat-related requests
Socket.IO WebSocket connection visible in Network tab
WebSocket error occurs when Etap attempts to block/inject
root_cause_analysis.recommendation: wrtn requires WebSocket-aware warning injection, not SSE-based. The openai_compat_sse envelope may not work. Consider: (1) WebSocket frame inspection/injection, (2) HTTP upgrade interception, (3) Alternative approach specific to Socket.IO protocol.

notes: Phase 6 FAIL confirmed. wrtn's chat architecture (Socket.IO WebSocket) fundamentally differs from other services (HTTP POST + SSE). The hold code removal build (etap-root-260417) is not the cause - the issue is architectural mismatch between APF envelope and wrtn's actual protocol.
